title: Accessibility Framework Skill Criterion
description: Validates per-criterion YAML items authored under .github/skills/accessibility/<framework-id>/items/<id>.yml for itemKind 'criterion'. The shape captures W3C WCAG-style success criteria (id, title, conformance level, principle, guideline, summary, applicability, verification mode, technique references). Identifiers are lower-kebab with optional dotted segments to permit either '1-1-1' or '1.1.1' style ids.

id.description: Lower-kebab criterion identifier (dots permitted for hierarchical ids).

itemKind.description: Discriminator marking this item as a criterion.

level.description: Conformance level for WCAG-style frameworks. Hosts MAY ignore for non-WCAG accessibility frameworks.

principle.description: Top-level principle the criterion belongs to (for WCAG: '1 Perceivable', '2 Operable', '3 Understandable', '4 Robust').

guideline.description: Parent guideline within the principle (for WCAG: '1.1 Text Alternatives', etc.).

addedIn.description: Framework version that introduced the criterion (for WCAG: '2.0', '2.1', '2.2').

summary.description: Normative paraphrase of the criterion. Authors MUST NOT fabricate; paraphrase the canonical source.

verificationMode.description: How the criterion is verified: automated (tooling alone), hybrid (tooling plus human review), manual (human only).

automatableBy.description: Capability identifiers (typically referencing capability-inventory-* items) that can automate detection.

techniques.description: URLs to canonical technique documents (e.g., W3C WCAG Techniques).

relatedAriaPatterns.description: Cross-walk identifiers to ARIA Authoring Practices Guide patterns or sibling Framework Skills.

references.description: Authoritative references (canonical specification URL is required).
